layout.text:
This is a demo configuration showcasing the capabilities of the MacroHero extension system. Explore the various pages to see examples of how to use different extensions and UI components to enhance your Owlbear Rodeo experience.

Each page demonstrates a different extension with sample code and interactive buttons. Use this as a starting point to create your own custom macros and interfaces!
To start, you can go to the parameters ⚙️ and change the `token` in the Global Variables to one of your tokens, then explore the ConditionMarkers, OwlTrackers, and StatBubbles pages to see dynamic token interactions. Also, check out the Token Helpers tool while you're at it!

Read data from any Google Spreadsheet using the Sheets REST API (read-only, API key required).

Before using this page, go to **Settings ⚙️ → Google Sheets** and enter your API key.

The three fields below define **what** to read:
- **Sheet ID** — the long identifier in the spreadsheet URL: `https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/**{SHEET_ID}**/edit`
- **Page name** — the tab name at the bottom of the spreadsheet (e.g. `Sheet1`, `Inventory`)
- **Range** — a cell or range in A1 notation (e.g. `A1`, `B2:D5`, `G26:I26`)

`getValue` returns the first cell of the range. `getRange` returns the full 2D array (or a flat 1D array for single-column ranges).

children.label: Get Value
children.tooltip: Fetch the first cell of the range and store it in `gsResult`
onclick: const val = await GoogleSheets.getValue(gsSheetId, gsSheetName, gsRange);

children.label: Get Range
children.tooltip: Fetch the full range and display it as JSON in `gsResult`
onclick: const rows = await GoogleSheets.getRange(gsSheetId, gsSheetName, gsRange);

layout.text:
# WARNING
Those features are not currently implemented in ConditionMarkers extension, please don't use them until they are released. This page is a preview of upcoming features and may contain placeholders.
Apply status conditions to tokens. Conditions persist on the token and can have numeric values.
Create and manage numeric trackers on tokens. Useful for HP, resources, cooldowns, and custom counters.
Manage health, AC, and temporary HP using the StatBubbles extension. Displays as bubbles on token.
Apply colored status rings around tokens for visual status indicators. Multiple rings can stack.

children.label: Remove All Rings
onclick: for (const ring of rings) { await ColoredRings.removeRing(token, ring); }

layout.text: Manage combat initiative using PrettySordid extension. Track turn order and active combatants.
Roll dice and display results to all players. Supports standard notation like 2d6+3.

Persist data across sessions using `Local`. Values survive page reload and browser restarts — they are stored in the browser's `localStorage` under a single `macroHero_localStorage` key.

Available methods:
- `Local.value(key, default)` — read a value (returns `default` if not set)
- `Local.set(key, value)` — write a value (any type: string, number, object…)
- `Local.keys()` — list all stored keys
- `Local.clear()` — wipe everything
Write any key/value pair and read it back.
A counter whose value survives page reload. `persistentCounter` is an `eval` variable — it reads `Local.value('persistentCounter', 0)` on load.
`Local.set` accepts any JSON-serializable value. Use objects to bundle related state.

children.label: Read tank HP
children.tooltip: Reads party.tank from the saved snapshot

children.label: Clear All
children.tooltip: Wipes all keys from Local storage

layout.text: Add atmospheric effects to the scene. Choose from rain, snow, fog, and other weather patterns to enhance immersion and set the mood.

The Weather extension allows you to apply dynamic environmental effects to your map. You can control:
- **Type** — the weather pattern ('SNOW', 'RAIN', 'SAND', 'FIRE', 'CLOUD', 'BLOOM', 'ENERGYSTORM', 'WATER', 'CURRENT')
- **Speed** — how fast the effect moves (1-4, default 1)
- **Density** — intensity or coverage (1-4, default 3)
- **Tint** — color overlay in hex format (e.g., `#4a6fa5` for blue tone)

Weather effects are visible to all players in the scene.
Warning: ENERGYSTORM, WATER, CURRENT effects and Tint parameter are only availaible use [Weather-extended](https://github.com/IAmConorNolan/weather-extended) version of the extension.

layout.label: Map ID
layout.placeholder: Paste your map item ID here

layout.text: Apply a preset weather type with default settings (speed 1, density 3). All effects are immediately visible to players.
Apply weather with custom speed, density, and color tints for more dramatic effects:
Query, check, and clear active weather effects on your map. Use these to manage or verify current conditions.

children.label: Get Weather
children.tooltip: Displays current type, speed, and density

children.label: Check Weather
children.tooltip: Yes/No check: is there active weather?

layout.text:
Craft intense atmospheric moments by combining weather effects with custom parameters:
- **Intense Storm**: Maximum speed and density for dramatic effect
- **Update Speed**: Change how fast weather moves without restarting
- **Update Tint**: Overlay a color to shift the mood (e.g., red for danger, blue for cold)

These tools let you escalate or de-escalate tension in real-time.

children.label: Intense Storm
children.tooltip: Maximum intensity: speed 4, density 4, blue tint

children.label: Update Tint
onclick: await Owlbear.notification.show('Weather tint updated to red', 'INFO');

layout.text: Apply dramatic visual effects using Embers. Chain effects together to create complex spell sequences with precise timing and positioning.

Embers uses a **fluent API** — you chain method calls to build effect sequences:

```
await Embers.sequence()
  .projectile(effectName, from, to, options)
  .aoe(effectName, targetLocation, options)
  .then()  // Wait for previous effects to finish
  .delay(milliseconds)  // Pause between effects
  .cast({ spellName: 'Name' })  // End sequence and display to players
```

**Core Methods:**
- **`.sequence()`** — Start a new effect sequence
- **`.projectile(name, from, to, opts)`** — Draw projectile from point A to point B
- **`.aoe(name, location, opts)`** — Area-of-effect burst at a location
- **`.cone(name, from, direction, opts)`** — Cone-shaped blast from origin
- **`.then()`** — Synchronization point: wait for all previous effects to complete before continuing
- **`.delay(ms)`** — Pause the sequence for N milliseconds
- **`.cast({ spellName })`** — Finalize sequence and display to all players

**Effect Timeline:**

1. **Without `.then()`** — Effects play simultaneously (in parallel)
   - `Embers.sequence().projectile(...).aoe(...).cast()` → projectile and AOE at same time

2. **With `.then()`** — Effects wait for previous ones to finish (sequential)
   - `Embers.sequence().projectile(...).then().aoe(...).cast()` → projectile plays first, then AOE

3. **With `.delay(ms)`** — Add a fixed pause
   - `Embers.sequence().projectile(...).delay(500).aoe(...).cast()` → wait 500ms after projectile

**Parameters:**
- `effectName` — Name of effect from Embers library (e.g., `'magic_missile'`, `'fireball.explosion'`, `'generic.impact.fire'`)
- `from` / `to` / `location` — Token IDs or positions on map
- `options` — Effect config: `{ copies: 3, size: 5, speed: 2, color: '#ff0000' }` etc.
- `spellName` — Display label in chat/log
Effects execute at the same time. Perfect for multi-target spells or simultaneous impacts.
Effects execute one after another. Use `.then()` to create dramatic sequences: projectile travels, then hits, then AOE explodes.

children.label: Projectile → Impact
children.tooltip: Missile travels first, then explodes on arrival

layout.text: Build complex multi-step sequences by combining projectiles, AOEs, delays, and synchronization points.

children.label: Chain Lightning
children.tooltip: 3 projectiles hit in sequence, each with an impact effect

children.label: Dual Projectiles + AOE
children.tooltip: 2 projectiles arrive simultaneously, then single AOE

layout.text:
Common effect names available in Embers:

**Projectiles:** `magic_missile`, `lightning`, `arrow`, `fireball`, `frost_bolt`, `shadow_bolt`

**AOE/Impacts:** `fireball.explosion`, `generic.impact.fire`, `generic.impact.electric`, `lightning.impact`, `frost.impact`, `shadow.impact`, `healing.light`

**Cones:** Various cone variants by direction

Explore the full library [here](https://embers.armindo.eu/listings) and experiment with different combinations to create unique spell effects for your game!
Type your command in the input below and execute it to test custom Embers sequences. Use `token` as caster and/or `await Token.getSelected()` for interactive selection.
Apply visual auras to tokens and items. Useful for spell effects, status indicators, or environmental effects.

layout.label: Red Glow
onclick: await Auras.addAura(token, { style: 'Glow', color: '#ff0000', animated: true });

layout.text: Control time-of-day lighting effects on maps. Adjust color, brightness, and saturation.
Display announcements to all players in the room. Supports Markdown formatting.

layout.label: Show Announcement
onclick: await Announcement.setAnnouncement({ active: true, content: '**Attention!** Important announcement.' });

layout.text: Display user feedback messages. Use appropriate variants for context.

children.label: Info
onclick: await Owlbear.notification.show('This is an info message', 'INFO');

children.label: Success
onclick: await Owlbear.notification.show('This is a success message', 'SUCCESS');

children.label: Warning
onclick: await Owlbear.notification.show('This is a warning message', 'WARNING');

children.label: Error
onclick: await Owlbear.notification.error('This is an error message');

children.label: Default
onclick: await Owlbear.notification.show('This is a default message', 'DEFAULT');

layout.text: Complete guide to all available UI components. Each element type is demonstrated below with descriptions and properties.
Large heading text for section organization. Supports custom colors via the 'color' property.
Regular paragraph text for descriptions, instructions, or information. Displays with word wrapping.
This is example text. You can use it to provide context, instructions, or any informational content to the user. Text elements automatically wrap to fit the container width.

**Bold text** and *italic text* and ~~strikethrough~~ and `inline code`
- List item one
- List item two
---
^^^ See that `---` line?
[See Marked (markdown parser) documentation](https://marked.js.org/)
**Counter value: ${counterValue}** — `${varName}` expressions work inside Markdown too, updated live.
Display a variable value with a label. Read-only, useful for showing dynamic information.
Clickable button that executes commands. Supports optional color, tooltip, and right-click actions.
Tooltips support **plain text**, **Markdown**, and raw HTML. `${varName}` expressions are evaluated at hover time. Hover the buttons below.

children.label: Markdown tooltip
children.tooltip: **Counter :** ${counterValue} - *Left-click* to confirm - *Right-click* to reset `setValue('counterValue', 0)`

layout.text:
Buttons support right-click (onrightclick) for alternate actions. Perfect for dual-action buttons like Heal/Damage, Cast/Cancel, etc.
Try LEFT-clicking and RIGHT-clicking the buttons below to see different behaviors:

children.label: Add Ring / Remove Ring
children.tooltip: Left: Add blue ring | Right: Remove

layout.text:
Button properties summary:
- onclick: Array of commands executed on LEFT-click
- onrightclick: Array of commands executed on RIGHT-click (optional)
- label: Button text
- color: Button background color (hex code)
- tooltip: Hover text — plain text, Markdown, or HTML. `${varName}` expressions evaluated at hover time.
Numeric input with +/- buttons. Configure step size, min/max, and custom colors.
Toggle boolean value. Can be displayed inline or in rows. Supports custom colors.
Text input field. Supports placeholder text and optional input validation via onupdate.
Select one value from a list. Supports simple string options or complex objects with labels and values.

Horizontal container that displays child elements in a row. Auto-distributes space equally.
Example row with 3 buttons:
Vertical container for stacking elements. Optionally add a border and accent color.

children.text: This stack has a blue border and blue accent color.

layout.text:
Grid of square buttons in a specified number of columns. Perfect for spell bars or inventory layouts.
Example: 4-column spell bar
Matrix properties: columns (number), buttonSize (pixels), gap (spacing), border (true/false), color (accent color).
Each matrixButton supports: icon (emoji or image URL), label (optional), color, borderColor, tooltip, onclick, onrightclick.
Icon only (no label), label only (no icon), or both. Icon can be an emoji or a URL pointing to an image.

matrixButton properties: icon (emoji or https://… URL), label, tooltip, color, borderColor, onclick, onrightclick. - icon only → large icon, no label beneath - label only → text centered in the button - icon + label → icon above, label below - icon as URL → renders an (24×24). Works with any http/https image or relative path containing a dot. - color → button background; borderColor → overrides the border independently - onrightclick → alternate action on right-click (same as regular buttons) empty onclick [] → button is rendered disabled at 50% opacity
Visual separator line. Used to organize sections without any additional properties.

- title: text, color
- text: text (plain, Markdown — **bold** *italic* ~~strike~~ `code` [link](url) - list --- hr, or raw HTML with <tags>; ${varName} works in all modes)
- value: var, label
- button: label, onclick, tooltip, color, onrightclick
- counter: var, label, step, color, onupdate
- checkbox: var, label, color, onupdate
- input: var, label, placeholder, onupdate
- dropdown: var, label, options, onupdate
- row: children (array of elements)
- stack: children, border, color
- matrix: columns, buttonSize, gap, border, color, children
- divider: (no properties)
